12.q: Knocking out of RBOHD in pumpkin by CRISPR/Cas9 editing resulted in lower root apex H2O2 and K+ content and GRF12, AHA1, and HAK5 expression, ultimately resulting in a salt-sensitive phenotype.

18.q: Y2H analysis revealed... yeast co-transformed with AD-CmoDREB2A/BD-CmoNAC1 were able to grow... LCI... GST Pull-down... CmoDREB2A-His was pulled down by CmoNAC1-GST... BiFC confirms the in vivo interaction between CmoDREB2A and CmoNAC1.

19.q: Y2H assay demonstrated robust growth of yeast co-transformed with CmoHKT1;1-Cub and CmoBI1L-Nub on selective SD/-LWHA media; co-immunoprecipitation and BiFC assays confirmed direct physical interaction between CmoBI1L and CmoHKT1;1.

20.q: CmoBI1L interacted with CmoNHX4, an essential Na+/H+ antiporter localized on the vacuole membrane.

23.q: JA content analysis revealed a significant decrease in JA levels in C/KOAOC2 and an increase in C/OEAOC2 compared with C/EV under salt stress, further supporting the role of CmoAOC2 in mediating CmoCDPK20-induced JA biosynthesis.

24.q: CmoCDPK20 interacts with and phosphorylates the transcription factor dehydration-responsive element-binding protein 2A (CmoDREB2A) at Ser241/396 residues.

31.q: This study shows that RBOHD-dependent H2O2 signaling in the root apex is important for pumpkin salt tolerance and suggests a novel mechanism, namely RBOHD-mediated transcriptional and post-translational activation of plasma membrane H+-ATPase operating upstream of HAK5 K+ uptake transporters.
